sample_note: Representative sample — Stripe uses POST for both create and update (no PUT/PATCH)

summaries.POST /v1/customers/{customer}/subscripti: Update a subscription on a customer

descriptions.DELETE /v1/accounts/{account}:
With Connect, you can delete accounts you manage.

Test-mode accounts can be deleted at any time.

Live-mode accounts that have access to the standard dashboard and Stripe is responsible for negative account balances cannot be deleted, which includes Standard accounts.
descriptions.DELETE /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accou: Delete a specified external account for a given account.
descriptions.GET /v1/accounts: Returns a list of accounts connected to your platform via Connect.
descriptions.GET /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_accounts: Retrieve a specified external account for a given account.
descriptions.GET /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities: Returns a list of capabilities associated with the account.
descriptions.GET /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities/: Retrieves information about the specified Account Capability.
descriptions.GET /v1/balance: Retrieves the current account balance, based on the authentication that was used to make the request.
descriptions.GET /v1/balance_transactions: Returns a list of transactions that have contributed to the Stripe account balance (e.g., charges, transfers, and so forth).
descriptions.GET /v1/balance_transactions/{id}:
Retrieves the balance transaction with the given ID.

Note that this endpoint previously used the path /v1/balance/history/:id.
descriptions.GET /v1/charges: Returns a list of charges you’ve previously created.
descriptions.GET /v1/charges/{charge}: Retrieves the details of a charge that has previously been created.
descriptions.GET /v1/customers/{customer}/sources/{id: Retrieve a specified source for a given customer.
descriptions.GET /v1/customers/{customer}/subscriptio: You can see a list of the customer’s active subscriptions.
Retrieves the subscription with the given ID.
descriptions.GET /v1/payment_intents/{intent}: Retrieves the details of a PaymentIntent that has previously been created.
descriptions.POST /v1/account_links: Creates an AccountLink object that includes a single-use Stripe URL that the platform can redirect their user to in order to take them through the Connect Onboarding flow.
descriptions.POST /v1/account_sessions: Creates a AccountSession object that includes a single-use token that the platform can use on their front-end to grant client-side API access.
descriptions.POST /v1/accounts: With Connect, you can create Stripe accounts for your users.
descriptions.POST /v1/accounts/{account}: Updates a connected account by setting the values of the parameters passed.
descriptions.POST /v1/accounts/{account}/bank_account: Create an external account for a given account.
descriptions.POST /v1/charges: This method is no longer recommended—use the Payment Intents API to initiate a new payment instead.
descriptions.POST /v1/charges/{charge}: Updates the specified charge by setting the values of the parameters passed.
descriptions.POST /v1/charges/{charge}/capture:
Capture the payment of an existing, uncaptured charge that was created with the capture option set to false.

Uncaptured payments expire a set number of days after they are created (7 by default), after which they are marked as refunded and capture attempts will fail.

Don’t use this method to capture a PaymentIntent-initiated charge.
descriptions.POST /v1/charges/{charge}/refunds:
When you create a new refund, you must specify a Charge or a PaymentIntent object on which to create it.

Creating a new refund will refund a charge that has previously been created but not yet refunded.
descriptions.POST /v1/customers/{customer}: Updates the specified customer by setting the values of the parameters passed.
descriptions.POST /v1/customers/{customer}/sources:
When you create a new credit card, you must specify a customer or recipient on which to create it.

If the card’s owner has no default card, then the new card will become the default.
descriptions.POST /v1/customers/{customer}/subscripti: Creates a new subscription on an existing customer.
Updates an existing subscription on a customer to match the specified parameters.
descriptions.POST /v1/payment_intents:
Creates a PaymentIntent object.

After the PaymentIntent is created, attach a payment method and confirm to continue the payment.
descriptions.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}:
Updates properties on a PaymentIntent object without confirming.

Depending on which properties you update, you might need to confirm the PaymentIntent again.
descriptions.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/cancel: You can cancel a PaymentIntent object when it’s in one of these statuses: requires_payment_method, requires_capture, requires_confirmation, requires_action or, in rare cases, processing.
descriptions.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/captur:
Capture the funds of an existing uncaptured PaymentIntent when its status is requires_capture.

Uncaptured PaymentIntents are cancelled a set number of days (7 by default) after...
descriptions.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/confir: Confirm that your customer intends to pay with current or provided payment method.

notes.GET /v1/account: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton — no collection path exists, summary says 'Retrieve account'. Heuristic misclassifies as list because no path parameter.
notes.GET /v1/balance: Audit: label=fetch confirmed. Singleton — no collection path exists, summary says 'Retrieve balance'. Heuristic misclassifies as list because no path parameter.
notes.POST /v1/accounts/{account}: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update an account'. Stripe uses POST for updates — operationId PostAccountsAccount lacks CRUD keyword but summary is unambiguous.
notes.POST /v1/accounts/{account}/capabilities: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update an Account Capability'. Same Stripe POST-as-update pattern.
notes.POST /v1/charges/{charge}: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update a charge'. Same Stripe POST-as-update pattern.
notes.POST /v1/charges/{charge}/capture: Action endpoint — captures a charge (state transition)
notes.POST /v1/charges/{charge}/refunds: Creates a refund sub-resource (POST to nested collection = create)
notes.POST /v1/customers/{customer}: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update a customer'. Same Stripe POST-as-update pattern. Heuristic sees POST to item path but operationId PostCustomersCustomer has no CRUD keyword.
notes.POST /v1/customers/{customer}/subscripti: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update a subscription'. Same Stripe POST-as-update pattern.
notes.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}: Audit: label=update confirmed. Summary says 'Update a PaymentIntent'. Same Stripe POST-as-update pattern.
notes.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/cancel: Action endpoint — cancels a payment intent
notes.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/captur: Action endpoint — captures a payment intent
notes.POST /v1/payment_intents/{intent}/confir: Action endpoint — confirms a payment intent
notes.POST to /{id}: Stripe uses POST for updates (same pattern as Twilio). POST to a resource with an ID is update, POST to a collection is create.
